sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Athletic Club is in strong form, sitting 2nd in the league with 9 points from 3 wins, scoring 6 goals in their last 5 matches. Deportivo Alavés, positioned 12th with 4 points, has struggled, managing only 1 win and scoring 3 goals in their recent games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, each team won 1-0 at home, indicating tight contests. Athletic Club has historically dominated with 3 wins in the last 5 matchups against Alavés.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Athletic Club excels in creating big chances (7, ranked 7th) and converting them (57.14%, ranked 2nd), while Alavés struggles offensively. Defensively, Athletic has conceded fewer goals (3 vs Alavés' 3), ranking higher in clean sheets and opponent big chances created.

sections.subtitle: Match Dynamics
sections.text: Athletic Club's high corner count (7.67 per game, ranked 1st) and home advantage at San Mamés suggest they will dominate possession and create more scoring opportunities. Alavés’ tendency to concede first (5/7 games) could be exploited by Athletic’s late scoring trend.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Athletic Club and Deportivo Alavés at San Mamés ended in a goalless draw, with neither side managing to break the deadlock. Athletic Club dominated much of the play, holding a significant advantage in possession and creating more entries into the final third and penalty area. Their statistical edge in expected goals suggests they were the more threatening side, though they failed to convert their opportunities into goals. Deportivo Alavés, on the other hand, struggled to create meaningful chances, with minimal impact in the attacking third and a much lower expected goals tally. Both teams showed discipline defensively, with each receiving just one yellow card.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Athletic Club's dominance
halftimeSections.text: Athletic Club clearly controlled the tempo of the first half, enjoying a lion’s share of possession and consistently penetrating the final third. Their high number of penalty area entries indicates a persistent attacking intent, though their finishing lacked the necessary precision. Players like Gorka Guruzeta showed promise in front of goal, leading in expected goals for his team, while midfielders such as Iñigo Ruiz de Galarreta provided stability with strong defensive contributions. The home side’s ability to win duels, both on the ground and in the air, further underlines their physical superiority in this half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Deportivo Alavés' defensive resilience
halftimeSections.text: Deportivo Alavés adopted a more conservative approach, focusing on defensive solidity rather than attacking ambition. Their limited final third entries and penalty area presence reflect a lack of offensive output, with no shots on target recorded in the first half. However, their defensive organization was commendable, as they managed to frustrate Athletic Club’s attacking efforts. Goalkeeper Antonio Sivera stood out with a strong performance, earning high marks for his composure under pressure. Despite being outplayed, Alavés held their ground and kept the scoreline level.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw a clear shift in momentum favoring Athletic Club for the majority of the time, with sustained periods of pressure in the opponent’s half. They had numerous attacking spells, particularly in the latter stages of the half, where they consistently built dangerous moves. Deportivo Alavés had fleeting moments of momentum, with brief attacking surges around the middle of the half, but these lacked the intensity or frequency to seriously challenge the home defense. Overall, the home side dictated the rhythm of the game with their forward pushes.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Athletic Club is likely to continue their aggressive approach, leveraging their home advantage and superior first-half stats to push for a breakthrough. They will need to sharpen their finishing to capitalize on the chances they are expected to create. Deportivo Alavés, meanwhile, may look to counterattack more effectively, potentially exploiting any overcommitment from the hosts. However, their lack of attacking threat in the first half suggests they will prioritize defending deep and looking for set-piece opportunities. Substitutions could play a key role, especially for Athletic Club, who might introduce fresh legs to maintain their intensity.
